title: Residual Income
event: Reyes Co.'s East Division earned $45,000 on average invested assets of $300,000. The company's hurdle rate is 10%. Compute residual income.

computationPaths.narration: Residual income asks: how much did the division earn ABOVE the minimum the capital demands?

memorize.body: Residual income = divisional income − (invested assets × hurdle rate).
RI fixes ROI's blind spot: a 15%-ROI division rejects a 12% project even when 12% beats the 10% hurdle. RI says take it.

distractors.feedback: That's raw income — the capital charge hasn't been deducted. RI nets out the required 30,000: 15,000.
That's the REQUIRED return itself. Residual income is the excess ABOVE it: 15,000.
